body.title: [escalated] memory_emit.MEMORY_ROOT frozen at import — UU_MEMORY_ROOT redirect ignored after import
body.description:
**Problem:** devlab/claudecode/memory_emit.py:38 binds `MEMORY_ROOT = os.environ.get('UU_MEMORY_ROOT', <repo>/devlab/runtime/memory)` as a MODULE-LEVEL constant at import time. Every OTHER reader (unseen_university/memory_root.memory_root(), proof_store) resolves UU_MEMORY_ROOT LIVE at call time. Consequence: any process that sets UU_MEMORY_ROOT AFTER importing memory_emit writes to the OLD (real) store while readers read the NEW store — a silent split. Surfaced in T-builder-merge-time-proof: a pytest fixture set UU_MEMORY_ROOT after collection-time import, so emit_merge_proof wrote 2 stray proofs into the REAL devlab/runtime/memory/proofs (cleaned up); the test had to monkeypatch memory_emit.MEMORY_ROOT directly as a workaround.

**Fix shape:** make memory_emit resolve the root LIVE — a `def _memory_root()` reading os.environ (or reuse unseen_university.memory_root.memory_root()) at each write, instead of the frozen module constant. Keep the default fallback identical.

**Affected files:** devlab/claudecode/memory_emit.py (MEMORY_ROOT usage at line ~154 out_dir).

**Completion test / Test plan:** red->green — set UU_MEMORY_ROOT AFTER importing memory_emit, emit one record, assert it lands under the NEW root (fails today: lands under the frozen default). 

**Scope boundary:** memory_emit root resolution only. NOT a broader store-path refactor.

**intention:** I intend that memory_emit honors UU_MEMORY_ROOT set at any time, so tests and tools can't silently split the store.
